HELIOS - HomogEneous and Layered medIa Optical Scattering
C++ surface integral equation (SIE) code for homogeneous and layered media scattering

SimJob Class Reference
Modules » Driver & Jobs

High-level driver for SIE simulations and field evaluation. More...

#include <SimJob.h>

 Destructor: frees owned domains and LUT if allocated.

 Allocate and zero a new solution vector x, and append to solVectors.

 XML-like job reader.

 Temp storage during load.
 
bool isCasimirJob
 Determine if this is a Casimir job.
 
LookupTableBin * t
 Look-up table for the periodic Green's function computation.
 
bool thereIsALookupTable
 True if there is a lookup table.
 

Static Protected Attributes

static int threads = 1
 Worker threads for field eval.
 

Detailed Description

High-level driver for SIE simulations and field evaluation.

Workflow: 1) LoadFromFile() parses the job file: mesh, materials, sources, etc. 2) CreateLSEMatrix() allocates the A matrix; domains fill it via the formulations (PMCHWT). 3) For each subjob: CreateLSEVec() and CreateSolVec(), fill excitation vectors, then SolveLSE() to get the solution current vector(s). 4) Optionally FieldEval() to sample fields or power/cross sections.

Definition at line 43 of file SimJob.h.

◆ absorptionCS()

double SimJob::absorptionCS ( Domain * domain)

Absorption cross-section.

Parameters
domainDomain over which to compute \(\sigma_{\alpha}\).
Returns
Absorption cross section.
Note
Not always accurate (volumetric methods based on Ohmic losses have better accuracy).

◆ CreateLSEMatrix()

int SimJob::CreateLSEMatrix ( )

Allocate and zero the system matrix A (and gradients if enabled).

Matrix size is 2Ne x 2Ne, where Ne is mesh edge count.

Returns
0 on success.

◆ EandHField()

std::pair< std::pair< cvec, cvec >, std::pair< cvec, cvec > > SimJob::EandHField ( rvec pos,
Domain * contDomain )

Return both \((\mathbf{E}\), \(\mathbf{H})\) incident and secondary pairs at pos.

Parameters
posCartesian position.
contDomainContaining domain.
Returns
\(\{\{\mathbf{E}_\mathrm{inc}(pos), \mathbf{H}_\mathrm{inc}(pos)\}, \{\mathbf{E}_\mathrm{scat}(pos), \mathbf{H}_\mathrm{scat}(pos)\}\}\).

◆ FieldEval()

int SimJob::FieldEval ( std::string jobFile,
std::string posFile,
std::string outPrefix,
uint fields,
bool needsCS = false )

Evaluate fields on a set of points read from a file.

Writes one or more output files (depending on fields bitmask):

  • .ein/.esc, .hin/.hsc, .din/.dsc, .bin/.bsc, .pin (Poynting), .csc (CS).
Parameters
jobFileJob file path.
posFilePosition list (CSV of x y z) or empty to skip.
outPrefixPrefix for output filenames.
fieldsBitmask: EFIELD/HFIELD/DFIELD/BFIELD/POYVEC/POWER.
needsCSIf true, writes cross sections to .csc.
Returns
0 on success; non-zero on failure.

Definition at line 861 of file SimJob.cpp.

Here is the call graph for this function:
Here is the caller graph for this function:

◆ FieldEvalParallel()

void SimJob::FieldEvalParallel ( std::vector< rvec > & posvec,
bool needsE,
bool needsH,
std::vector< std::pair< std::pair< cvec, cvec >, std::pair< cvec, cvec > > > & result,
std::vector< Domain * > & contDom,
int & nPoint,
std::mutex & mut )

Thread worker to evaluate fields over posvec in parallel.

Parameters
posvecInput positions.
needsEWhether to compute \(\mathbf{E}\).
needsHWhether to compute \(\mathbf{H}\).
resultOutput array of \(\{\{\mathbf{E}_\mathrm{inc}, \mathbf{H}_\mathrm{inc}\}, \{\mathbf{E}_\mathrm{scat}, \mathbf{H}_\mathrm{scat}\}\}\) per point.
contDomPre-computed containing domains for each point.
nPointShared index (guarded by mut).
mutMutex for index progress + periodic status prints.

◆ LoadFromFile()

int SimJob::LoadFromFile ( )

Parse a full \(<job>...</job>\) block from the already-open JobParser.

Returns
0 on success; non-zero on parse/consistency error.

Parse a full \(<job>...</job>\) block (mesh, domains, wavelength, etc.). Handles both periodic and non-periodic jobs and ensures consistency (e.g., number of domains <= mesh->MaxDomainIndex()).

int SimJob::Simulate ( std::string jobFile,
int solverType,
std::string outPrefix )

High-level entry: run a simulation for a given job file.

jobFilePath to job file.
solverTypeSolver selection (as in SolveLSE()).
outPrefixPrefix for output filenames.

0 on success; non-zero on any failure.

◆ SolveLSE()

int SimJob::SolveLSE ( int solverType)

Solve the current linear system with the chosen solver.

Parameters
solverType0: direct; 1: cgsqr; 2: LU (default).
Returns
0 on success; non-zero on failure.

Solve linear system with selected solver backend. (cgsqr / LU via external FORTRAN or BLAS/LAPACK calls.)
